title: Consecration.
content:
Proud of my broken heart since thou didst break it,
    Proud of the pain I did not feel till thee,
    Proud of my night since thou with moons dost slake it,
    Not to partake thy passion, my humility.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "Consecration.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
